title: All About Bugs Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
rounds.explanation: butterfly: An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The body parts that some insects and birds use for flying.
rounds.explanation: wings: The body parts that some insects and birds use for flying.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: In a higher place than something else, without touching it.
rounds.explanation: above: In a higher place than something else, without touching it.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A small jumping insect that makes a chirping sound with its wings.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The body parts that some insects and birds use for flying.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To move through the air, usually by using wings.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The butterfly landed on the red flower to drink nectar.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The spider built a web between two branches.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Butterflies have colourful wings, but ants do not always have wings.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the simple present tense correctly?
rounds.explanation: There is only one spider, so the verb takes an s. The other sentences leave the s off, have no helping word, or add the s to the naming word instead.

rounds.prompt: Choose the correct verb for this fact: “Bees ______ honey.”
rounds.explanation: Bees is more than one, so the verb stays plain. Makes belongs with one bee, made is the past, and making needs a helping word.

rounds.prompt: Which question asks about the place where spiders live?
choices: How do spiders spin a web?
rounds.explanation: Where always asks about a place. What asks about a thing, How asks about a way of doing something, and Why asks for a reason.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: How many legs do all insects have, and what are their three main body parts called?
choices: Six legs. The three main body parts are the head, the thorax and the abdomen.
The bug is on the speaker, because the poem says, “There's a bug on me.”
rounds.answer: Six legs. The three main body parts are the head, the thorax and the abdomen.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Six legs. The three main body parts are the head, the thorax and the abdomen.

rounds.prompt: What was in Grandpa's pocket, and where had he found it?
choices: The bug is on the speaker, because the poem says, “There's a bug on me.”
Stay calm and do not wave your arms, and then the bee will leave you alone.
A cricket, and he found it under a stone in the garden.
rounds.answer: A cricket, and he found it under a stone in the garden.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: A cricket, and he found it under a stone in the garden.

rounds.prompt: Adam told Amal how to stay safe near the bee. What did he tell her to do?
choices: She counted its legs one by one and found eight, and insects have only six.
Stay calm and do not wave your arms, and then the bee will leave you alone.
“I don't know.” Nobody in the poem can say where the bug went.
rounds.answer: Stay calm and do not wave your arms, and then the bee will leave you alone.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Stay calm and do not wave your arms, and then the bee will leave you alone.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Name and Describe a Bug
Speaking Game — Bug Riddles
rounds.target: It has six legs. It can fly. It makes honey.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — My Garden Tour
rounds.target: Here is a bee. It collects nectar and it makes honey.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The butterfly is on the flower.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The bee is above the flower.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The worm can crawl through the soft, wet soil.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'anthill' mean in this unit?
choices: The home that ants build, often with many rooms under the ground.
An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.
rounds.answer: The home that ants build, often with many rooms under the ground.
rounds.explanation: anthill: The home that ants build, often with many rooms under the ground.

rounds.prompt: What does 'fly' mean in this unit?
choices: An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.
To move through the air, usually by using wings.
rounds.answer: To move through the air, usually by using wings.
rounds.explanation: fly: To move through the air, usually by using wings.

rounds.prompt: What does 'ant' mean in this unit?
choices: A small animal with eight legs that spins webs to catch insects.
A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.
An insect with large, colourful wings that flies from flower to flower.
rounds.answer: A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.
rounds.explanation: ant: A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'in'?
rounds.explanation: in is used as a position in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'chirp'?
rounds.explanation: chirp is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'insect'?
rounds.explanation: insect is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A small insect that lives in large groups and works with others.
A small animal with eight legs that spins webs to catch insects.
The body parts that animals use for walking, standing or jumping.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The home that ants build, often with many rooms under the ground.
Touching the top or the surface of something else.
In the middle of two other things, with one on each side.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: To move through the air, usually by using wings.
To move slowly along the ground or across a surface.
To gather things together and bring them to one place.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Why is a spider not an insect?
choices: It has eight legs, and insects have six.
It has wings, and insects have no wings.
It lives in the soil, and insects live in trees.
It makes honey, and insects do not make honey.
rounds.answer: It has eight legs, and insects have six.
rounds.explanation: Counting the legs is the test. Insects have six legs, so a spider with eight legs belongs to a different family of animals.

rounds.prompt: A butterfly is sitting and touching the top of a flower. Which position word is correct?
rounds.explanation: On means touching the top of something. Under means below it, between needs two things, and above means higher up without touching.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells you that the ant has gone inside its home?
choices: The ant is on the anthill.
The ant is above the anthill.
The ant is in the anthill.
The ant is in front of the anthill.
rounds.answer: The ant is in the anthill.
rounds.explanation: In means inside. On means touching the top, above means higher up, and in front of means just before it.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which of these is a small animal with eight legs that spins a web?
rounds.explanation: A spider has eight legs and spins a web. A cricket is a six-legged insect, nectar is a sweet drink and an anthill is a home.

rounds.prompt: Which insect collects nectar from flowers and makes honey?
rounds.explanation: A bee collects nectar and makes honey. A web is what a spider spins, above is a position word and crawl is an action word.

rounds.prompt: How many legs does every insect have?
rounds.explanation: All insects have six legs. Two legs belong to a bird, eight legs belong to a spider, and a worm is the creature with no legs.
